_howToUpdate: Edit this file only. Set 'resultDeclared' to true and fill 'officialResultDate' the moment CBSE issues an official notice. Update 'lastChecked' whenever you verify against cbse.gov.in. Commit and push — the app reads this file fresh (cache-busted) every time it's opened or checked.

exam.name: CBSE Class 10 Second Board Examination (Phase 2 / Session 2), 2026
exam.context: First-ever CBSE two-board-exam cycle for Class 10 under NEP 2020. Phase 2 lets students improve up to 3 subjects or clear compartment; the higher of Phase 1 / Phase 2 marks counts.

expectedResultWindow.note: No written CBSE notification has fixed an exact date yet. This range is built only from on-record statements by CBSE officials and CBSE's own published two-exam-system guidelines, not from unofficial rumours.

statements.source: CBSE Controller of Examinations, Dr. Sanyam Bhardwaj (public webinar on the two-board-exam system)
statements.statement: Said the board was targeting 30 June to declare the Class 10 second board exam result and close the cycle before Class 11 admissions begin.
statements.reliability: On-record official statement, not yet a written CBSE notice/circular

statements.source: CBSE circular on the two-examination scheme
statements.statement: States that results of the second examination will be declared in the month of June.
statements.reliability: Official CBSE circular language, but does not give an exact date

howToCheckResult: Roll Number, School Number, Admit Card ID and Date of Birth (from your admit card) are required.
Visit cbseresults.nic.in and open the 'Secondary School Examination Results (Phase II)' link.
Or open DigiLocker / UMANG app and look under issued education documents.
Do not trust any site or message asking for payment, OTP, or that isn't one of the four official sources above.
